consistency.class_tag: Consistent with standard D&D 5e class feature naming conventions for Paladin.
consistency.level_tag: Level 1 is standard for many core class features.
consistency.feature_type: Subclass features in 5e are generally marked 'is_subclass_feature': true, although Dueling is a Fighting Style, which is often selected at level 1 and sometimes categorized differently depending on the source compilation, but the tag is mechanically acceptable.

quality.description_clarity: The description is clear and directly mirrors the Dueling Fighting Style text.
quality.naming: Name 'Dueling' is accurate for the associated mechanic.

mechanics.rule_integrity: Mechanically sound; it grants a +2 bonus to damage rolls when meeting the specific one-handed weapon requirement. This is a standard Fighting Style.
mechanics.potential_issues: The RAW data incorrectly tags this as a subclass feature ('is_subclass_feature': true). Dueling is a Fighting Style, typically chosen at level 1 (or level 2 for base classes like Fighter/Ranger, but Paladins get it via Variant Human/Custom Lineage or as a dedicated choice if the source material groups them this way). In official 5e rules, Fighting Styles are usually distinct from Paladin subclass (Oath) features. This tag might be contextually specific to the database structure but is mechanically inaccurate regarding official class progression.
mechanics.effect_validation: The effect (+2 damage with a single one-handed melee weapon) is correctly stated.

recommendations.suggestion: Set to false, as Dueling is a Fighting Style, not an Oath feature. If the system strictly categorizes all level 1 choices outside the main chassis ability as 'subclass', this tag might be acceptable within that system's context, but flag for review.

analysis.final_assessment: High quality description and mechanics, but the 'is_subclass_feature' tag is likely miscategorizing a standard Fighting Style.
